summary: ICIS Vietnam PP yarn/inj rose 20 USD to 990-1,020 USD/ton CFR
Hanwha sub-1,000 lots sold out; Petrochina/Jinneng hold 1,000-1,020 CIF
Wide domestic offer gap after Friday cuts; imported homo 31,500-33,000 VND/kg
paras: According to ICIS pricing, PP yarn/injection prices in Vietnam rose by 20 USD/ton to 990–1,020 USD/ton/CFR. However, aside from the fixed PP price increase in Vietnam, PP yarn/injection/block copo and random copo prices in Southeast Asia last week mostly moved sideways to down by an additional 10–20 USD/ton compared to the prior week.
Import demand remains low as processing units focus on consuming existing raw material inventory while also shifting to local sources to meet essential needs at more attractive price levels.

The market is showing growing contradictions and increasing tensions. Some suppliers are hesitant to lower offer prices due to shrinking profit margins. However, some buyers believe the downward price trend may continue, especially if crude oil prices drop sharply in the event of a US–Iran peace deal. Supply pressure is expected to be maintained as producers in South Korea and Southeast Asia continue to operate at high capacity, and the Prefchem Malaysia complex is restarting one of its two PP production lines.

Notably, cheap PP shipments from Hanwha South Korea (previously offered at PP homo 990 USD/ton/CIF, PP copo at 1,020 USD/ton/CIF) have been fully sold out and are no longer available. This indicates that when PP homo falls below the 1,000 USD/ton/CIF mark, buyers accept imports to replenish production inventory. PP yarn/injection offers from Petrochina and Jinneng (China) to Vietnam remain at 1,000–1,020 USD/ton/CIF, unchanged from late last week.
Overall, the decline in import PP prices is showing signs of slowing. Some grades like PP random are even seeing short-term price recovery as supply has not fully shifted to an oversupply state.

Due to the rapid price decline toward the end of last week, there is a fairly wide gap in offer prices among distributors. For PP 114G/105G, commercial companies in the north are offering to retail customers in small quantities at 30,800–31,500 VND/kg incl. VAT; some distributors offer wholesale prices at 30,500–30,800 VND/kg incl. VAT, with reports of transactions as low as 30,000 VND/kg incl. VAT.

Other domestic factories have not yet adjusted prices this morning following last week's sharp cuts. In the south, PP yarn T3034 from BSR and F501 from Hyosung Vina are offered at a low of 30,300–30,800 VND/kg incl. VAT.

For imported PP homo, offer prices in the southern market commonly range from 31,500–33,000 VND/kg incl. VAT. PP yarn HP550J from Jinneng (China) is being offered by a major distributor at 31,400 VND/kg incl. VAT. In the north, some Chinese-origin PP yarn items are trading at the lowest level of 30,000 VND/kg incl. VAT.

PP copo offer prices have not seen many adjustments today. However, for PP copo off-grade, Stavian today offered some items from SK Chemical (South Korea) at 38,500 VND/kg incl. VAT, down 500 VND/kg from late last week.

summary: ICIS: LDPE -100 to 1,100-1,130; LLDPE -50 to 950-980 USD/ton CFR
HDPE Film -90 to 940-1,000; HDPE injection -105 to 950-1,050 CFR
Today LLDPE Film 7042/7050 cut to 28,700-29,000 VND/kg in the north
paras: According to ICIS pricing published for the last trading session of the previous week (July 3), the LDPE price list in Vietnam dropped sharply by 100 USD/ton to 1,100–1,130 USD/ton/CFR; LLDPE fell by 50 USD/ton to 950–980 USD/ton/CFR; HDPE Film fell by 90 USD/ton in the low segment and by 50 USD/ton in the high segment to 940–1,000 USD/ton/CFR; HDPE injection fell by 105 USD/ton to 950–1,050 USD/ton/CFR; HDPE blow moulding dropped sharply by up to 200 USD/ton to 900–1,000 USD/ton/CFR.

PE import negotiations in China received some positive signals as offer prices for US-origin goods declined compared to the previous week. Market participants forecast that US supply will continue to decrease in the coming period. In the Southeast Asian PE import market, supply from the Middle East has improved, although prices at all levels face pressure due to shipping logistics and transportation shortages. Most buyers still maintain a cautious attitude amid concerns about potential delays caused by price volatility. However, demand for spot deliveries is also expected to remain at low levels in July.

Late last week, the domestic PE market saw many sellers making sharp price adjustments, especially for shipments arriving in September–October 2026. In the north, many sellers had already lowered offer prices for LLDPE Film FD21HS from Qamar, 218WJ from Sabic Arabia to 30,400–30,700 VND/kg incl. VAT. One distributor of LLDPE Film FD21HS from Qamar with September 2026 shipments offered at the much lower level of 27,100–27,200 VND/kg incl. VAT.

Today (July 6), some northern traders have lowered offer prices for LLDPE Film 7042/7050 from China to 28,700–29,000 VND/kg incl. VAT for shipments arriving late July. LLDPE prices have thus fallen below the 30,000 VND/kg incl. VAT mark for the first time since mid-March.

Similarly for HDPE, some traders today lowered HDPE blow offers (China/US origin) to 29,000–30,000 VND/kg incl. VAT, down 2,000–3,000 VND/kg from late last week. These are among the items seeing the strongest price adjustments early this week.

As for LDPE and some other HDPE items, domestic offer prices have not yet seen significant adjustments compared to late last week. The price gap among suppliers is quite large. For instance, LDPE Film from the US/China is offered at around 38,000 VND/kg incl. VAT, while LDPE Film from the Middle East is around 40,000–41,000 VND/kg incl. VAT.

summary: China PVC futures rose 6.34 USD/ton today; stabilizing since late June
No new China price-cut offers to Vietnam in ~10 days
Yihua SG-5 holds lowest at 20,200 VND/kg (Yen Vien, Hanoi)
paras: Futures trading prices on the Chinese market today (July 6) rose across the board, with PVC up 6.34 USD/ton compared to last week's settlement price. In reality, PVC prices in China have shown a tendency to stabilize since late June, with very few distributors accepting further price cuts. Last week, according to Platts pricing, the PVC price list in China and Northeast Asia fell only slightly by 5 USD/ton.

Over the past ten days or so, there have been no additional PVC price-cut offers from Chinese suppliers to Vietnam. The lowest offer is PVC SG-5 from Yihua (China) at the Yen Vien station area (Hanoi), holding at 20,200 VND/kg incl. VAT — unchanged from late June.

PVC market developments in Vietnam last week moved from sideways to declining, but declines were mainly in the high-priced goods segment. Early this week, few sellers have adjusted prices further. The base PVC price list is commonly at 21,000–22,500 VND/kg incl. VAT.

Some market participants say they are waiting for a new offer from a major Taiwanese factory, expected to be announced in mid-July, to better determine the short-term PVC price trend. Nevertheless, in actual trading, downward pressure on PVC prices remains.
